stories.headline: Iran Keeps Up Attacks Before US Deadline, Dimming Peace Chances
stories.summary: Iran pressed on with attacks across the Persian Gulf hours before US President Donald Trump’s deadline to agree to a ceasefire plan, clouding prospects for a breakthrough in efforts to end the war.

stories.headline: Brent Oil Is Stressed, Not Broken, by War, Morgan Stanley Says
stories.summary: The growing stress in the oil market triggered by the Middle East war is showing up in huge premiums for prompt real-world barrels, with the wider Brent complex working as it should, according to Morgan Stanley.

stories.headline: How Goldman Sachs Dodged a Private Credit Exodus
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Group says its private credit fund narrowly escaped a broader exodus due to its reliance on institutional investors. Goldman Sachs Private Credit, which manages a so-called non-traded business development company, met redemption requests in the first quarter amounting to 4.999% of its outstanding shares. That contrasts with peers including Blue Owl Capital that saw redemption requests dramatically higher than an industry-wide 5% lim

stories.summary: Home prices in Toronto fell to their lowest level in more than five years as an outlook for slow economic growth and broader global turmoil make prospective buyers cautious.

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
